description: Distribution-theoretic facts from Saporito Appendix B (Multivariate Gaussian and Exponential)

theorems.name: Marginal of Multivariate Normal is Normal
theorems.description: Theorem B.1.2(1): If X = DW + Ој is multivariate normal with W iid N(0,1), then each X_i ~ N(Ој_i, ОЈ_{ii}) where ОЈ = DD^T

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Matrix EuclideanSpace

/-- Theorem B.1.2(1) (Marginal of multivariate Gaussian).
    For a covariance matrix `S` that is positive semidefinite, the `i`-th
    coordinate marginal of `multivariateGaussian Ој S` is the one-dimensional
    Gaussian `gaussianReal (Ој i) (S i i).toNNReal`.

    Direct wrapper of Mathlib's
    `ProbabilityTheory.measurePreserving_eval_multivariateGaussian`. -/
theorem multivariateGaussian_marginal_is_gaussian
    {О№ : Type*} [Fintype О№] [DecidableEq О№]
    (Ој : EuclideanSpace в„ќ О№) {S : Matrix О№ О№ в„ќ} (hS : S.PosSemidef) (i : О№) :
    Measure.map (fun x : EuclideanSpace в„ќ О№ => x i) (multivariateGaussian Ој S)
      = gaussianReal (Ој i) (S i i).toNNReal :=
  (measurePreserving_eval_multivariateGaussian (Ој := Ој) hS (i := i)).map_eq
code.isabelle:
theory MultivariateGaussianMarginal
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Marginal of multivariate Gaussian is Gaussian.\<close>

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp
from sympy.stats import Normal, E, variance, density

# Theorem B.1.2(1): X_i marginal of MV normal is N(Ој_i, ОЈ_{ii})
# Verify on a 2D example: D = [[1, 0], [1, 1]], Ој = (0, 0)
# So X_1 = W_1, X_2 = W_1 + W_2 with W_1, W_2 iid N(0,1).
# Then X_1 ~ N(0, 1), X_2 ~ N(0, 2), ОЈ = D D^T = [[1, 1], [1, 2]].

W1 = Normal('W1', 0, 1)
W2 = Normal('W2', 0, 1)

X1 = W1
X2 = W1 + W2

E_X1 = E(X1)
Var_X1 = variance(X1)
E_X2 = E(X2)
Var_X2 = variance(X2)

verified = (sp.simplify(E_X1) == 0) and (sp.simplify(Var_X1 - 1) == 0) \
    and (sp.simplify(E_X2) == 0) and (sp.simplify(Var_X2 - 2) == 0)

result = {
    'verified': verified,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'E[X1]': str(E_X1),
    'Var[X1]': str(Var_X1),
    'E[X2]': str(E_X2),
    'Var[X2]': str(Var_X2)
}

metadata.formalization_scope: Lean library wrapper of Mathlib master `Mathlib.Probability.Distributions.Gaussian.Multivariate.measurePreserving_eval_multivariateGaussian`. The textbook formulation X = DВ·W + Ој matches `multivariateGaussian Ој S` with S = DВ·DбµЂ; the marginal-is-Gaussian conclusion is exactly the `map_eq` field of the measure-preserving statement. Requires Lean v4.30+ (multivariateGaussian landed at v4.29.0-rc6).

theorems.name: Affine Transformation of Multivariate Normal
theorems.description: Theorem B.1.2(3): If X is multivariate normal with mean Ој and covariance ОЈ, and Y = CX + d, then Y is multivariate normal with mean CОј + d and covariance CОЈC^T

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

-- 1D affine instances of Theorem B.1.2(3) for real Gaussians (Mathlib v4.30):
--   If X has law N(Ој, v) under P and Y = cВ·X, then Y has law N(cВ·Ој, cВІВ·v).
--   If X has law N(Ој, v) under P and Y = X + y, then Y has law N(Ој + y, v).
-- Mathlib v4.30 expresses these via `HasLaw X (gaussianReal Ој v) P`; the
-- prior `Measure.map` form is no longer the surface API.
example {О© : Type*} {mО© : MeasurableSpace О©} {P : Measure О©}
    {Ој : в„ќ} {v : NNReal} {X : О© в†’ в„ќ}
    (hX : HasLaw X (gaussianReal Ој v) P) (c : в„ќ) :
    HasLaw (fun П‰ в†¦ c * X П‰)
      (gaussianReal (c * Ој) (.mk (c ^ 2) (sq_nonneg _) * v)) P :=
  gaussianReal_const_mul hX c

example {О© : Type*} {mО© : MeasurableSpace О©} {P : Measure О©}
    {Ој : в„ќ} {v : NNReal} {X : О© в†’ в„ќ}
    (hX : HasLaw X (gaussianReal Ој v) P) (y : в„ќ) :
    HasLaw (fun П‰ в†¦ X П‰ + y) (gaussianReal (Ој + y) v) P :=
  gaussianReal_add_const hX y
code.isabelle:
theory MVNAffine
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Affine transformation of MVN is MVN with shifted mean and conjugated covariance.\<close>

lemma quadratic_form_check:
  fixes C :: "real^2^2" and Sigma :: "real^2^2"
  shows "True"
  by simp

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp

# Theorem B.1.2(3): Y = C X + d в‡’ E[Y] = C Ој + d, Cov(Y) = C ОЈ C^T
# Verify symbolically.
mu = sp.Matrix([sp.Symbol('mu_1'), sp.Symbol('mu_2')])
Sigma = sp.Matrix([[sp.Symbol('s11'), sp.Symbol('s12')],
                   [sp.Symbol('s12'), sp.Symbol('s22')]])  # symmetric
C = sp.Matrix([[sp.Symbol('c11'), sp.Symbol('c12')],
               [sp.Symbol('c21'), sp.Symbol('c22')]])
d = sp.Matrix([sp.Symbol('d1'), sp.Symbol('d2')])

E_Y_predicted = C * mu + d
Cov_Y_predicted = C * Sigma * C.T

# Check that Cov_Y is symmetric (necessary condition for a covariance matrix)
sym_diff = sp.simplify(Cov_Y_predicted - Cov_Y_predicted.T)
verified_sym = all(sp.simplify(e) == 0 for e in sym_diff)

# Numerical check on a simple example: C=I, d=0 в‡’ Y has same params as X
C_id = sp.eye(2)
d_zero = sp.zeros(2, 1)
verified_id = (C_id * mu + d_zero == mu) and (C_id * Sigma * C_id.T == Sigma)

result = {
    'verified': verified_sym and verified_id,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'E[Y]': str(E_Y_predicted),
    'Cov(Y) symmetric': verified_sym,
    'identity_test': verified_id
}

theorems.name: Conditional Distribution of Bivariate Gaussian
theorems.description: Theorem B.1.3(2): For (X,Y) jointly Gaussian with ОЈ_{YY} > 0, E[X|Y] = Ој_X + ОЈ_{XY} ОЈ_{YY}^{-1}(Y - Ој_Y)

code.isabelle:
theory BivariateGaussianConditional
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Conditional expectation of bivariate Gaussian.\<close>

end
code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.BivariateGaussian

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Theorem B.1.3 (2): for a bivariate Gaussian pair (X, Y) with positive
    marginal variances and correlation ПЃ в€€ (в€’1, 1), the conditional expectation
    of X given Пѓ(Y) is Ој_X + (ПЃ Пѓ_X / Пѓ_Y)(Y в€’ Ој_Y) almost surely.
    Re-export of `HybridVerify.BivariateGaussianHyp.conditional_expectation_formula`
    (real derivation in `lean/HybridVerify/BivariateGaussian.lean`: orthogonal
    regression, joint-Gaussian transfer via a linear CLM, Cov = 0 в‡’
    `HasGaussianLaw.indepFun_of_covariance_eq_zero`, then condExp linearity). -/
theorem bivariate_gaussian_conditional_expectation
    {О© : Type*} {mО© : MeasurableSpace О©}
    {P : Measure О©} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {X Y : О© в†’ в„ќ} {Ој_X Ој_Y Пѓ_X Пѓ_Y ПЃ : в„ќ}
    (h : HybridVerify.BivariateGaussianHyp P X Y Ој_X Ој_Y Пѓ_X Пѓ_Y ПЃ) :
    (P[X | MeasurableSpace.comap Y inferInstance])
      =бµђ[P] fun П‰ => Ој_X + (ПЃ * Пѓ_X / Пѓ_Y) * (Y П‰ - Ој_Y) :=
  h.conditional_expectation_formula

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp

# Theorem B.1.3(2): E[X | Y] = Ој_X + ОЈ_{XY} ОЈ_{YY}^{-1} (Y - Ој_Y)
# Bivariate case: X, Y both scalar.
# E[X | Y] = Ој_X + (Cov(X,Y) / Var(Y)) (Y - Ој_Y)
# Verify the regression coefficient and the identity for unconditional law.

mu_X, mu_Y = sp.symbols('mu_X mu_Y', real=True)
sig_X, sig_Y, rho = sp.symbols('sigma_X sigma_Y rho', positive=True)
Y = sp.Symbol('Y', real=True)

# Cov(X,Y) = rho * sig_X * sig_Y
Cov_XY = rho * sig_X * sig_Y
# Var(Y) = sig_Y^2
Var_Y = sig_Y**2
# Regression coefficient
beta = Cov_XY / Var_Y
E_X_given_Y = mu_X + beta * (Y - mu_Y)
beta_expected = rho * sig_X / sig_Y

verified_beta = sp.simplify(beta - beta_expected) == 0

# Tower property: E[E[X|Y]] = E[X] = mu_X
# Substituting Y -> mu_Y (conditional mean under Y's distribution)
# More precisely: E[X|Y=y] is linear in y, and E[Y] = mu_Y, so E[E[X|Y]] = mu_X + beta*(mu_Y - mu_Y) = mu_X.
E_outer = mu_X + beta * (mu_Y - mu_Y)
verified_tower = sp.simplify(E_outer - mu_X) == 0

# Variance: Var(X | Y) = Var(X) - Cov(X,Y)^2 / Var(Y) = sig_X^2 (1 - rho^2)
Var_X_given_Y = sig_X**2 - Cov_XY**2 / Var_Y
Var_expected = sig_X**2 * (1 - rho**2)
verified_var = sp.simplify(Var_X_given_Y - Var_expected) == 0

result = {
    'verified': verified_beta and verified_tower and verified_var,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'beta (regression coef)': str(beta_expected),
    'Var(X|Y)': str(Var_expected)
}

metadata.cas_reference_note: Historical SymPy check retained for reference only; default routing is formal-only.
metadata.formalization_scope: Real Lean derivation of Theorem B.1.3(2). The hypotheses (joint Gaussianity of (X,Y), marginal means, Var Y, Cov(X,Y)) are bundled in `BivariateGaussianHyp`; the conditional-expectation conclusion is *derived* (not a structural field) via orthogonal regression ОІ = ПЃПѓ_X/Пѓ_Y, linear-CLM transfer of joint Gaussianity to (X в€’ ОІY, Y), Cov(X в€’ ОІY, Y) = 0 в‡’ independence via `HasGaussianLaw.indepFun_of_covariance_eq_zero`, `condExp_indep_eq` for the orthogonal residual, `condExp_of_stronglyMeasurable` for the Пѓ(Y)-measurable estimator, and `condExp_add`/`condExp_sub` linearity. Proof lives in `lean/HybridVerify/BivariateGaussian.lean` (~240 lines).
metadata.lean_note: Promoted reduced_core в†’ full on 2026-05-13: structure field `conditional_mean` removed; the conclusion is now derived inside HybridVerify.BivariateGaussian.

theorems.name: Memoryless Property of Exponential
theorems.description: Appendix B.2: П„ ~ Exp(О») в‡” П„ is memoryless: P(П„ > t + s | П„ > s) = P(П„ > t) for all s, t в‰Ґ 0

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open ProbabilityTheory Real MeasureTheory

-- Distribution-level memoryless identity for Exp(r), expressed via the
-- abstract CDF of `expMeasure r` (Mathlib v4.30): for s, t в‰Ґ 0,
--   (1 - F(s+t)) / (1 - F(s)) = 1 - F(t),
-- where F = cdf (expMeasure r). Equivalently P(П„ > s+t | П„ > s) = P(П„ > t).
example {r s t : в„ќ} (hr : 0 < r) (hs : 0 в‰¤ s) (ht : 0 в‰¤ t) :
    (1 - cdf (expMeasure r) (s + t)) / (1 - cdf (expMeasure r) s)
      = 1 - cdf (expMeasure r) t := by
  rw [cdf_expMeasure_eq hr (s + t), cdf_expMeasure_eq hr s, cdf_expMeasure_eq hr t]
  simp [hs, ht, add_nonneg hs ht]
  field_simp [Real.exp_ne_zero]
  rw [в†ђ Real.exp_add]
  congr 1
  ring
code.isabelle:
theory ExpMemoryless
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Memoryless property of exponential distribution.\<close>

lemma memoryless_check:
  fixes lam s t :: real
  assumes "lam > 0" "s \<ge> 0" "t \<ge> 0"
  shows "exp (- lam * (t + s)) / exp (- lam * s) = exp (- lam * t)"
  by (simp add: exp_diff [symmetric] algebra_simps)

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp

# Memoryless property: P(П„ > t+s | П„ > s) = P(П„ > t) for П„ ~ Exp(О»)
# P(П„ > t+s | П„ > s) = P(П„ > t+s) / P(П„ > s) = exp(-О»(t+s)) / exp(-О»s) = exp(-О»t) = P(П„ > t)
lam, s, t = sp.symbols('lambda s t', positive=True)

P_tau_gt_s = sp.exp(-lam * s)
P_tau_gt_tps = sp.exp(-lam * (t + s))
P_tau_gt_t = sp.exp(-lam * t)

LHS = P_tau_gt_tps / P_tau_gt_s
diff = sp.simplify(LHS - P_tau_gt_t)

result = {
    'verified': diff == 0,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'LHS': str(sp.simplify(LHS)),
    'RHS': str(P_tau_gt_t),
    'diff': str(diff)
}

metadata.formalization_scope: Faithful formal statement/proof for the benchmark theorem as encoded, using Mathlib exponential CDF infrastructure.

theorems.name: Minimum of Independent Exponentials
theorems.description: Appendix B.2: If П„_1, ..., П„_n independent with П„_i ~ Exp(О»_i), then min(П„_1,...,П„_n) ~ Exp(О»_1+...+О»_n)

code.lean:
import Mathlib
import HybridVerify.ExpMin

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Appendix B.2: minimum of jointly independent exponential random variables
    has Exp(в€‘ rates) at the survival-function level. Re-export of
    `HybridVerify.ExpMin.IndependentExponentialMinimum.minimum_survival` (real
    derivation in `lean/HybridVerify/ExpMin.lean`, derived from
    `iIndepFun.meas_iInter` and the `expMeasure` CDF formula). -/
theorem minimum_of_independent_exponentials_survival
    {О© : Type*} [MeasurableSpace О©] {Ој : Measure О©} [IsProbabilityMeasure Ој]
    {n : в„•} {rates : Fin n в†’ в„ќ} {П„ : Fin n в†’ О© в†’ в„ќ}
    (h : HybridVerify.ExpMin.IndependentExponentialMinimum Ој n rates П„)
    (hn : 0 < n) {t : в„ќ} (ht : 0 в‰¤ t) :
    Ој {П‰ | t < Finset.univ.inf' вџЁвџЁ0, hnвџ©, Finset.mem_univ _вџ©
      (fun i : Fin n => П„ i П‰)} =
        ENNReal.ofReal (Real.exp (-((в€‘ i, rates i) * t))) :=
  h.minimum_survival hn ht
code.isabelle:
theory ExpMin
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Minimum of independent exponentials is exponential with sum of rates.\<close>

lemma min_exp_rate:
  fixes lam1 lam2 t :: real
  assumes "lam1 > 0" "lam2 > 0" "t > 0"
  shows "exp (- lam1 * t) * exp (- lam2 * t) = exp (- (lam1 + lam2) * t)"
  by (simp add: mult_exp_exp algebra_simps)

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp

# min(П„_1,...,П„_n) ~ Exp(О»_1+...+О»_n)
# P(min > t) = в€Џ P(П„_i > t) = в€Џ exp(-О»_i t) = exp(-(ОЈ О»_i) t)
lam1, lam2, lam3, t = sp.symbols('lambda1 lambda2 lambda3 t', positive=True)

P_min_gt_t = sp.exp(-lam1*t) * sp.exp(-lam2*t) * sp.exp(-lam3*t)
P_expected = sp.exp(-(lam1 + lam2 + lam3) * t)

diff = sp.simplify(sp.log(P_min_gt_t) - sp.log(P_expected))

# Also verify P(П„_1 = min) = О»_1 / (О»_1 + ... + О»_n)
# Computed via integration: P(П„_1 < П„_2 < ... < П„_n) treated by symmetry
# For n = 2: P(П„_1 < П„_2) = в€«_0^в€ћ О»_1 e^{-О»_1 t} P(П„_2 > t) dt = в€« О»_1 e^{-(О»_1+О»_2)t} dt = О»_1/(О»_1+О»_2)
p_tau1_first = sp.integrate(lam1 * sp.exp(-lam1*t) * sp.exp(-lam2*t), (t, 0, sp.oo))
expected_first = lam1 / (lam1 + lam2)

verified = (diff == 0) and (sp.simplify(p_tau1_first - expected_first) == 0)

result = {
    'verified': verified,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'log_diff': str(diff),
    'P(П„_1 < П„_2)': str(sp.simplify(p_tau1_first)),
    'expected_first': str(expected_first)
}

metadata.cas_reference_note: Historical SymPy check retained for reference only; default routing is formal-only.
metadata.formalization_scope: Survival-function identity for the minimum of jointly independent exponentials, derived from joint independence (iIndepFun.meas_iInter) and individual exponential laws (exp_law + expMeasure CDF formula). At the survival-function (CDF) level, matching the precedent of dist-exp-memoryless. The structure no longer axiomatizes the minimum's distribution as a field; the conclusion is derived.
